name: Sweden and Norway (1815–1844)

colors.symbolism: the union badge, taken to represent Norway

description: The Swedish flag — a blue field with a yellow Nordic cross — defaced in the upper-hoist canton with a union badge: a white saltire on a red square marking the union with Norway.
history: Common ensign of the United Kingdoms of Sweden and Norway, introduced from 1815 (a common civil/merchant ensign on this pattern followed in 1818). The Swedish blue-and-yellow cross flag was charged in the canton with a white saltire on red representing Norway, which had previously shared the Dano-Norwegian flag. It was used until the royal resolution of 20 June 1844, which replaced the simple badge with the quartered Swedish-Norwegian union mark (the 'sillsallad') denoting the equal status of the two kingdoms.

features.symbolism: the union with Norway; the red canton with a white saltire was taken to represent Norway
